candidates.row_label: Net Foreign Exchange Income - Business Revenue
candidates.why: Among candidates, this is explicitly labeled as business revenue, whereas 'Balancing Item - Revenue' is an adjustment line. Though FX is only a component of total income, it is the best semantic match to a revenue line in the restricted candidate set.

'Balancing Item - Revenue' appears to be an adjustment line used to true-up reported revenue. It is less semantically appropriate than a direct revenue component but kept as a fallback in case the FX revenue line is missing in some years.

final_choice.row_label: Net Foreign Exchange Income - Business Revenue

variables.notes: The true top-line appears to be 'Total income', but it is not in the candidate list. Selected the only line explicitly labeled as business revenue, plus the balancing item as backup. This will significantly understate actual revenue; manual review recommended.
No COGS-related candidates were provided; likely not separately disclosed for this bank, so treated as zero per instructions.

candidates.why: This is operating income, not an operating expense; therefore it should NOT be used as SG&A. Excluded per instructions.
This is also income, not expense; should not be treated as SG&A. Excluded.
Again clearly income, not expense; excluded from SG&A.
Personnel-related overhead, fits SG&A definition and is not a total/subtotal. Included as SG&A component.
Generic operating expense bucket, not labeled as total; included as SG&A overhead component.
Administrative overhead is classic SG&A. Included as SG&A component even though preview values are empty, per instructions.
Clearly operating expenses, not a labeled total and distinct from depreciation/impairments. Included as SG&A component.
Appears to be an additional bucket of operating overhead expenses. Included as SG&A component.

A generic operating expenses bucket that likely aggregates overhead where no explicit SG&A line is provided. It is not labeled as a total/subtotal and is included to ensure coverage of overhead per hierarchy rule (c).
Likely additional administrative overhead; included as SG&A component despite missing preview values.
Share-based comp is typically part of SG&A; included as SG&A component though amounts are not shown in preview.

variables.notes: Excluded income lines from SG&A and selected all non-total operating overhead expense buckets provided, as no single explicit SG&A line exists.
No R&D-related row labels in candidates; assumed no separate R&D disclosure.

candidates.why: Specific interest income/expense on bonds; could be part of interest expense mix but not the main consolidated line. Included as a lower-priority fallback.
Represents interest and fees on loans (likely income). Not pure interest expense; therefore placed after explicit expense lines in priority list.
This is the primary aggregate interest expense line in the income statement; best match for XINT and used as first priority.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses on liabilities to credit institutions
candidates.why: Component of interest expenses; used as fallback if the main 'Interest expenses' line is missing in some years.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses related to the issuance of securities
candidates.why: Another component of interest expenses; included as lower-priority fallback.
Interest on subordinated debt; also part of interest expense; included as fallback.
Net of interest income and expenses, not pure expense. Excluded to avoid mixing income and expense in XINT.
Tax-related, not interest expense itself; excluded.
Profit allocation, not interest expense; excluded.
Profit allocation, not interest expense; excluded.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Explicit interest expense component related to lease liabilities; included as second priority after the main interest expense line.
Label suggests bank interest payable; included as a very low-priority generic fallback in case used in some years.

candidates.row_label: Shares, units and other equity interests
candidates.why: Equity investment asset; not an interest expense line; excluded.
Balance sheet accrual, not P&L expense by itself; excluded.
Equity line, not interest expense; excluded.
Equity line, not interest expense; excluded.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
Interest expenses on liabilities to credit institutions
Interest expenses related to the issuance of securities

variables.notes: Priority list starts with the aggregate 'Interest expenses' line, then key specific expense components, then other interest-related lines as fallbacks. Net interest and non-expense items were excluded.

candidates.row_label: Attributable to Equity capital certificate holders
candidates.why: This is an income allocation line, not a balance sheet equity stock; excluded for BE.

candidates.row_label: Shares, units and other equity interests
candidates.why: This is an asset investment line (equity securities), not shareholders' equity; excluded.
Component of equity (share capital), but BE should use a total equity figure rather than a component. Left as backup implicitly via totals; not selected directly.
Another equity component; BE should use total equity line instead. Not selected.
Explicit total equity line, slightly different from 'Total equity'; used as secondary priority in case of different reporting periods.
Primary book equity line including all equity; best representation of BE. Selected as first priority.

variables.notes: Selected the total equity lines from the balance sheet, with lowercase 'Total equity' as primary and 'Total Equity' as backup, rather than individual components or income allocation lines.

candidates.why: This is the income statement allocation of profit to minorities, not the balance sheet minority equity; BE/MIB should use the stock on the balance sheet. Excluded for MIB stock.
Appears to mirror the income statement allocation; again a flow, not the stock. Excluded.

candidates.row_label: Shares, units and other equity interests
candidates.why: Asset investments, not minority equity in consolidated subsidiaries; excluded.
Balance sheet line for non-controlling interests; primary choice for MIB.
Alternative label for same concept in some periods; included as second priority.

variables.notes: Using balance sheet non-controlling interests/minority interest lines to represent MIB (minority equity).
